---
title: "About"
toc: false
---

I'm a workforce planning and capacity planning leader with 10+ years building the systems and models organizations use to answer "do we have enough people, in the right places, doing the right things?"

Most recently at Amazon, I owned long-range capacity planning for ~14,000 CDL drivers across 400+ sites and led global WFM for a 5,000-person organization. Before that: T-Mobile (led a Kronos/UKG rollout for 20,000 employees), Missouri Star Quilt Co, and a web analytics consultancy.

These days I'm focused on building the kind of tools I always wished existed — forecasting pipelines that pull their own data, dashboards that synthesize rather than just display, and planning tools that work without someone manually hitting refresh on a spreadsheet.

## What I build

- **Forecasting models** — demand, headcount, inventory, capacity. Primarily Prophet and AutoGluon. Built for accuracy, built to explain themselves to stakeholders.
- **Automated data pipelines** — connecting forecasting models to the sources they depend on, so the model reflects reality without manual intervention.
- **Decision-support tools** — dashboards and reports that surface what matters, flag what's changing, and give leaders something to act on.

## Background

**Education**  
MBA, Thunderbird School of Global Management  
BA Political Science, BYU–Hawaii (summa cum laude)

**Experience**  
Sr. PM, Demand Planning & Workforce Intelligence — Amazon  
Capacity Planning Manager — Amazon Transportation  
Business Operations Manager — T-Mobile  
Reporting & Analytics Manager — Missouri Star Quilt Co

## Get in touch

I'm open to conversations about workforce planning, forecasting, and operations analytics — whether that's a full-time role, consulting work, or just comparing notes.

**Email:** [your@email.com](mailto:your@email.com)  
**LinkedIn:** [linkedin.com/in/yourprofile](https://linkedin.com/in/yourprofile)  
**GitHub:** [github.com/yourusername](https://github.com/yourusername)
